description_long: This object is a rectangular stone temple with a classical colonnade of tall columns encircling its perimeter, each column featuring a red lower section and a pale stone upper section. The roof is pitched and covered with reddish-orange terracotta tiles, bordered by a blue decorative frieze with geometric motifs. The temple sits on a raised stone platform with steps leading up to a prominent front portico, which is framed by a triangular pediment. The entrance is open, allowing a view into the interior, which appears to have a tiled floor and ample space for movement. The exterior is adorned with minimal but bold color accents, and the structure is robust, evoking ancient Greek or Roman architectural traditions.
description: A rectangular stone temple with red and blue accents, surrounded by columns and topped with a pitched terracotta-tiled roof. The structure features a prominent columned entrance and sits on a raised stone platform.
description_view_0: Side view showing the long colonnade, blue frieze, and partial view of the open entrance and tiled floor.
description_view_1: Frontal view highlighting the main entrance with steps, triangular pediment, and symmetrical columns.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the uninterrupted colonnade and blue frieze, with no visible entrance.
description_view_3: Rear three-quarter view showing the back pediment, tiled roof, and continuous columns.
attribute_ornamentation: moderate density, geometric painted frieze, colored column bases, minimal sculptural detail
attribute_entrance_design: Columned portico with wide stone steps, framed by a triangular pediment and flanked by evenly spaced columns with painted bases
